header_caveat: _This is a synthetic discussion simulation. n=30 persisted society, 5 groups of 6 personas. Not representative of the California market. Not a forecast. Not a demand forecast. Not a launch verdict. The product is unlaunched — no persona has actually used it._
executive_summary: This is a synthetic discussion simulation across 66 run-scoped personas in 5 groups of 6.
No over-cooperation detected. Public/private distinction preserved.

discussion_setup.structure: 0 pre-ballot · 1 public opening · 2 challenge · 3 peer-response · 4 proof discussion · 5 reflection · 6 final ballot

public_opening_stances.speaker: Jules J.
public_opening_stances.text: I run most evenings, so anything pitched as a wearable light catches my eye — but I haven't seen LumaLoop in person, just the name. proof_demands.speaker: Quinn L.
proof_demands.proof_need: Independent durability + visibility test data at sub-zero temps with battery life numbers

proof_demands.speaker: Marlowe F.
proof_demands.proof_need: MSRP plus a side-by-side runtime/lumens/weight comparison against established competitors

proof_demands.speaker: Riley C.
proof_demands.proof_need: A short real-world demo showing the everyday use case and setup time.

proof_demands.speaker: Avery A.
proof_demands.proof_need: Independent visibility test (distance in meters, lumens, runtime) plus MSRP

proof_demands.speaker: Casey P.
proof_demands.proof_need: Transparent price and a side-by-side cost-per-lumen-hour vs Noxgear/Nathan.

proof_demands.speaker: Gray T.
proof_demands.proof_need: Independent side-by-side field test: lumens, runtime at high, weight on head, and bounce on a 10k night run.

proof_demands.speaker: Jordan N.
proof_demands.proof_need: A clear spec sheet: physical form, dimensions, materials, and how it integrates with existing kit.

proof_demands.speaker: Nico K.
proof_demands.proof_need: Side-by-side visibility distance test (meters seen by a driver) vs. a standard LED collar, plus battery runtime and weather resistance specs.

proof_demands.speaker: Tatum W.
proof_demands.proof_need: Side-by-side spec sheet: lumens, runtime, weight, IP rating, and price vs Sprinter.

proof_demands.speaker: Sage D.
proof_demands.proof_need: Side-by-side run footage and weight/battery specs vs. Noxgear Tracer2, ideally with a return window.

proof_demands.speaker: Logan J.
proof_demands.proof_need: Independent reflectivity/visibility test results (e.g., ANSI/ISO retroreflective rating) plus a meaningful sample of verified user reviews.

proof_demands.speaker: Maya C.
proof_demands.proof_need: Independent side-by-side: weight, bounce, runtime vs Xinglet and Tracer2

proof_demands.proof_need: Independent lumen/runtime specs and a side-by-side visibility test against a known benchmark like the BD Sprinter.

proof_demands.proof_need: Clear IPX rating plus runtime/lumens vs Nathan or Knuckle Lights

proof_demands.proof_need: A detailed spec sheet plus side-by-side night visibility footage vs. Tracer360.

proof_demands.speaker: Kerry K.

proof_demands.speaker: Ellis G.

proof_demands.speaker: Marlowe B.
proof_demands.proof_need: Independently measured lumens, runtime curve, and weight vs. a named competitor.

positioning_implications: A subset of personas would shift positively given the right proof material — focus marketing claims on what they asked for.
15 persona(s) actively resisted peer pressure — their dissenting reasoning is the contrarian signal worth validating with real users.
what_founder_should_test_next: Proof artifacts personas demanded most: lumens (x25), battery (x12), review (x5), durability (x4), ip rating (x2). Build small concept tests around each before scaling spend.
Talk to real prospects matching the resisting personas' profile — their objection pattern is the contrarian risk.
Validate this synthetic discussion against a small real-people discussion before treating any signal as load-bearing.
caveats: This is a synthetic discussion simulation, not a real focus group.
Not representative of the California market.
Personas are run-scoped and brief-scoped. No global personas.
Product 'LumaLoop' is unlaunched; no persona has actually used it.
Psychology trait values are simulation controls, not real psychological diagnoses.

rationale: aggregate=0.978 (anti_forecast=1.0, unlaunched=1.0, memory=1.0, private_public=1.0, interaction=1.0, profile_usage=1.0, non_overcooperation=1.0, stance_validity=1.0)
scale_reliability=0.975 reflection_completeness=0.924 cost_efficiency=1.0 persisted_personas=66/66 calls=5/462 transient_retries=0 failed_calls=0 est_cost=$0.09/cap=$20.00
